title: Megalastrum inaequale
intro: Megalastrum inaequale is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and oxidative stress, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Megalastrum inaequale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, Megalastrum inaequale is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Megalastrum inaequale is classified within the plant family Ophioglossaceae, which includes the genus Megalastrum. This genus is a type of fern, specifically a type of Ophioglossum fern. The species Megalastrum inaequale is native to South America. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is further classified within the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
